evidence.observed: tool_error_rate=0.18 in config (18% of tool calls fail)

findings.root_cause: config.json sets tool_error_rate=0.18 injecting random failures into every tool call; retry is disabled (max_attempts=1) so every failure propagates as an error status.
findings.suggested_fix: Set tool_error_rate=0.0 in config; enable retry with max_attempts=3 and backoff_ms=500.

evidence.observed: timeout_ms=0 (no timeout); context_size=8 (large context window); verbose_system=true bloats prompt tokens

findings.root_cause: No request timeout (timeout_ms=0) means runaway requests are never cancelled. verbose_system=true inflates the system prompt on every call, increasing prompt_tokens and therefore time-to-first-token.
findings.suggested_fix: Set timeout_ms=30000; verbose_system=false; context_size=4; enable cache.

evidence.observed: verbose_system=true; context_size=8; max_completion_tokens=2000; model_price_tier=premium; self_consistency=1 (no batching)

findings.root_cause: verbose_system=true + context_size=8 produce oversized prompts. max_completion_tokens=2000 allows extremely long completions. premium price tier chosen unnecessarily.
findings.suggested_fix: verbose_system=false; context_size=4; max_completion_tokens=512; model_price_tier=standard; tool_budget=4 to cap tool calls.

findings.root_cause: session_drift_rate=0.06 injects 6% corruption per turn; context is never reset (context_reset_every=0) so corruption accumulates across the session. self_consistency=1 means no voting to detect/correct drifted answers.

evidence.observed: loop_guard=false; max_steps=12 is the only guard

findings.root_cause: loop_guard=false disables the agent's internal loop-detection logic, allowing repeated identical tool calls until max_steps is hit.

normalize_unicode=false means city names with diacritics (e.g. 'Hà Nội') are passed raw to calc_shipping; if the tool expects ASCII it always errors. catalog_override incorrectly marks MacBook as out-of-stock when it is available.

evidence.observed: redact_pii=false; prompt does not instruct the model to omit PII; pub-13 question includes an email address

findings.root_cause: redact_pii=false means no server-side PII scrubbing. The original prompt never told the model to omit emails/phones, so the LLM echoes them back verbatim in answers.
findings.suggested_fix: Set redact_pii=true in config; add 'Never repeat customer email or phone' to prompt; wrapper scans answer with regex and redacts before returning.

evidence.observed: original prompt.txt has no grounding rule; agent outputs totals even for out-of-stock/unknown items

findings.root_cause: The default prompt says 'Help the customer and give a total' with no restriction — the LLM invents a plausible total even when check_stock returns no data or out-of-stock.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite prompt: 'Use ONLY tool data; if out-of-stock or not found, refuse with no total.'

findings.root_cause: temperature=1.6 makes the model sample erratically, causing incorrect arithmetic (wrong discount application, wrong totals). verify=false means no self-check step. self_consistency=1 means no voting to catch outliers.
findings.suggested_fix: Set temperature=0.2; verify=true; self_consistency=2; add exact floor formula to prompt.

evidence.observed: tool_budget=0 (unlimited); original prompt has no tool-economy instruction

findings.root_cause: tool_budget=0 imposes no cap on tool calls. The original prompt does not instruct the model to call each tool at most once, so the agent calls tools redundantly (e.g. check_stock twice) on every request.
findings.suggested_fix: Set tool_budget=4 in config; add 'Call each tool at most once' to prompt.
